cero: noun. A large scombroid food fish (Scomberomorus regalis) found chiefly in the West Indies.
clime: noun. A particular region defined by its weather or climate.
clomp: noun. The sound of feet hitting the ground loudly.
clop: noun. The sound of a horse 's shod hoof striking the ground.
coil: noun. Something wound in the form of a helix or spiral.
coiler: noun. A person who, or device which, coils.
coir: noun. The fibre obtained from the husk of a coconut, used chiefly in making rope, matting and as a peat substitute.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
compile: verb. To make by gathering pieces from various sources.
complier: noun. One who complies or obeys.
cop: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Coptic.
cope: verb. To deal effectively with something, especially if difficult.
copier: noun. A machine that copies graphical material; a duplicator.
cor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Cornish.
core: noun. In general usage, an essential part of a thing surrounded by other essential things.
corm: noun. A short, vertical, swollen, underground stem of a plant (usually one of the monocots) that serves as a storage organ to enable the
cormel: noun. A small corm that develops at the base of an existing corm.
crime: noun. A specific act committed in violation of the law, especially criminal law.
crop: noun. A plant, grown for it, or its fruits or seeds, to be harvested as food, livestock fodder, or fuel or for any other economic purpose.
elm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eleme.
emic: adjective. Of or pertaining to the analysis of a cultural system or its features from the perspective of a participant in that culture.
emir: noun. A prince, commander or other leader or ruler in an Islamic nation.
epic: noun. An extended narrative poem in elevated or dignified language, celebrating the feats of a deity, demigod (heroic epic), other legend
ice: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Icelandic.
imp: verb. To engraft or plant (a plant or part of one, a sapling, etc.).
impel: verb. To urge a person; to press on; to incite to action or motion via intrinsic motivation.
implore: verb. To beg or plead for (something) earnestly or urgently; to beseech.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
lie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likila.
lier: noun. A person or thing that lies, in the sense of being horizontal.
lime: noun. Any inorganic material containing calcium, usually calcium oxide (quicklime) or calcium hydroxide (slaked lime).
limp: verb. To walk lamely, as if favoring one leg.
limper: adjective. comparative form of limp: more limp
lop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lopa.
lope: verb. To travel an easy pace with long strides.
loper: noun. One who or that which lopes; a runner; a leaper.
lore: noun. All the facts and traditions about a particular subject that have been accumulated over time through education or experience.
mel: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Melanau.
melic: adjective. Of or pertaining to Greek lyric verse.
mir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isthmus Mixe.
mire: noun. Deep mud; moist, spongy earth.
moc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mocoví.
moil: verb. To toil, to work hard.
moiler: noun. One who moils, or works hard.
moire: noun. Originally, a fine textile fabric made of the hair of an Asiatic goat.
mole: noun. A naevus, a pigmented, slightly raised, and sometimes hairy spot on the skin.
oiler: noun. One who or that which oils.
ole: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Olekha.
oleic: adjective. Of or pertaining to oil, especially to vegetable oil.
omer: noun. A former small Hebrew unit of dry volume equal to about 2.3 L or 2.1 quarts.
ope: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Old Persian.
orc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orma.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
oriel: noun. A large polygonal recess in a building, such as a bay window, forming a protrusion on the outer wall.
orle: noun. A bordure that runs around the outline of a shield without touching the edge.
pec: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Pesisir.
peloric: adjective. Of a flower, having a mutation such that a normally asymmetric flower becomes radially symmetric.
per: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Persian.
peri: noun. A sprite or supernatural being.
peril: noun. A situation of serious and immediate danger.
pile: noun. A mass of things heaped together; a heap.
pom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Pomo.
pome: noun. A type of fruit in which the often edible flesh arises from the swollen base of the flower and not from the carpels.
pore: noun. A tiny opening in the skin.
price: noun. The cost required to gain possession of something.
prim: adjective. Of a person, their manner or appearance: Formal and precise; stiffly decorous.
prime: adjective. First in importance, degree, or rank.
prole: noun. A member of the proletariat; a proletarian.
recoil: noun. A starting or falling back; a rebound; a shrinking.
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
repo: noun. Clipping of repossession, most commonly of a vehicle, house or condominium.
rice: noun. Cereal plants, Oryza sativa of the grass family whose seeds are used as food.
riel: noun. The monetary unit of Cambodia, equivalent to 100 sen or 10 kak.
rile: verb. To stir or move from a state of calm or order.
rim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nyaturu.
rime: noun. Archaic in the form rimes: originally, any frozen dew forming a white deposit on exposed surfaces; hoar frost (sense 1).
rip: verb. To divide or separate the parts of (especially something flimsy, such as paper or fabric), by cutting or tearing; to tear off or out
ripe: adjective. Of a fruit, vegetable, seed, etc., ready for reaping or gathering; having attained perfection; mature.
rope: noun. Thick strings, yarn, monofilaments, metal wires, or strands of other cordage that are twisted together to form a stronger line.
